title: Egalitarian-equivalent and strategy-proof mechanisms in homogeneous multi-object allocation problems
abstract: arXiv:2507.09152v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: We study the problem of allocating homogeneous indivisible objects among agents with money. In particular, we investigate the relationship between egalitarian-equivalence (Pazner and Schmeidler, 1978), as a fairness concept, and efficiency under agents' incentive constraints. As a first result, we characterize the class of mechanisms that satisfy egalitarian-equivalence, strategy-proofness, individual rationality, and no subsidy. Our characterization reveals a strong tension between egalitarian-equivalence and efficiency: under these properties, objects are allocated only in limited cases. To address this limitation, we replace strategy-proofness with the weaker incentive property non-obvious manipulability (Troyan and Morrill, 2020). We show that this relaxation allows us to design mechanisms that achieve efficiency while still ensuring egalitarian-equivalence. Furthermore, under efficiency, we identify the agent-welfare-optimal mec

translated_title: 同质多物品分配问题中的平等等价与防策略机制
translated_abstract: 我们研究了在有货币的情况下将同质不可分割物品分配给代理人的问题。特别地，我们探讨了作为公平概念的平等等价（Pazner 和 Schmeidler，1978）与在代理人激励约束下的效率之间的关系。首先，我们刻画了满足平等等价、防策略性、个体理性和无补贴的机制类别。我们的刻画揭示了平等等价与效率之间的强烈张力：在这些性质下，物品仅在有限情况下被分配。为解决这一局限，我们将防策略性替换为较弱的激励性质——非明显可操纵性（Troyan 和 Morrill，2020）。我们证明，这种放宽允许我们设计出既能实现效率又能确保平等等价的机制。此外，在效率条件下，我们确定了代理福利最优的机制。
tldr: 本文揭示了平等等价与效率在防策略机制中的冲突，并通过引入非明显可操纵性设计出兼顾公平与效率的机制。
en_tdlr: This paper reveals the tension between egalitarian-equivalence and efficiency in strategy-proof mechanisms, and designs mechanisms that balance fairness and efficiency by introducing non-obvious manipulability.
